title: Automated Decision Audit Trail Reconstruction (Art. 12)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'EU AI Act' → 'Art.19'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.19 (Automatically generated logs) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.3: Automated Decision Audit Trail Reconstruction (Art. 12).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.3' running on index=ai_decision sourcetypes: airflow:task, ai:gateway, rules:engine — shared decision_id, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=ai_decision earliest=-24h decision_id=*
| eval hop=case(sourcetype="airflow:task",1,sourcetype="ai:gateway",2,sourcetype="rules:engine",3,true(),0)
| stats values(sourcetype) as path, min(_time) as t0, max(_time) as t1, range(_time) as span_sec by decision_id
| eval complete=if(mvcount(path)>=3,1,0)
| where complete=0
| eval span_min=round(span_sec/60,2)
| table decision_id, path, span_min, t0, t1
description: Chains decision_id across orchestrator, model gateway, and business rules engine to evidence end-to-end decision audit trail required for high-risk ADM logging.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_decision and sourcetypes ai:gateway, airflow:task, rules:engine. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Propagate decision_id across pipeline stages; (2) create macro `ai_decision_index`; (3) schedule every 30m for incomplete chains; (4) attach ITSI episode template for cross-team triage. Indexes required: `index=ai_decision`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:gateway`, `sourcetype=airflow:task`, `sourcetype=rules:engine`.
visualization: Sankey or sequence table (path), Histogram (span_sec), Table (incomplete decision_id)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for chains decision_id across orchestrator, model gateway, and business rules engine to evidence end-to-end decision audit trail required for high-risk adm logging. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_decision` sourcetypes: `airflow:task`, `ai:gateway`, `rules:engine` — shared decision_id.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Propagate decision_id across pipeline stages; (2) create macro `ai_decision_index`; (3) schedule every 30m for incomplete chains; (4) attach ITSI episode template for cross-team triage.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_decision earliest=-24h decision_id=*
| eval hop=case(sourcetype="airflow:task",1,sourcetype="ai:gateway",2,sourcetype="rules:engine",3,true(),0)
| stats values(sourcetype) as path, min(_time) as t0, max(_time) as t1, range(_time) as span_sec by decision_id
| eval complete=if(mvcount(path)>=3,1,0)
| where complete=0
| eval span_min=round(span_sec/60,2)
| table decision_id, path, span_min, t0, t1
```

Understanding this SPL

**Automated Decision Audit Trail Reconstruction (Art. 12) (EU AI Act)** — Chains decision_id across orchestrator, model gateway, and business rules engine to evidence end-to-end decision audit trail required for high-risk ADM logging.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_decision` sourcetypes: `airflow:task`, `ai:gateway`, `rules:engine` — shared decision_id. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_decision.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_decision, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hop** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **decision_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **complete** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where complete=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **span_min** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: complete, decision_id, hop, path, span_min, span_sec, t0, t1.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Sankey or sequence table (path), Histogram (span_sec), Table (incomplete decision_id)
